commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The branch marks memory-specific embedding provider registration as deprecated compatibility across SDK/docs metadata, adds non-bundled plugin-inspect notices, and adds guardrail tests for bundled legacy registrations.

Reproducibility: Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue? Yes for the review blocker by source inspection: PR-head status diagnostics check plugin metadata fields while runtime API registrations are stored separately in `registry.memoryEmbeddingProviders`.
commands.review_followup: A narrow automated repair can add runtime-registration notice coverage; maintainers still own the stacked API landing order.

target.title: Deprecate memory-specific embedding provider registration
target.body:
## Summary

- marks the memory-specific embedding provider registration seam as deprecated compatibility
- surfaces non-bundled `memoryEmbeddingProviders` / `registerMemoryEmbeddingProvider` usage through plugin compatibility notices
- updates SDK/docs and adds guardrails so new bundled plugins do not add more legacy memory embedding provider registrations

## Verification

Behavior addressed: old memory-specific embedding provider registration now remains compatible but is visible as deprecated for plugin authors.
Real environment tested: local OpenClaw worktree with a real external/workspace plugin inspected through `openclaw plugins inspect`; Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox on Linux (`tbx_01ks62sf86sqjq39v8f7qbs0fm`, Actions run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26250560922).
Exact steps or command run after this patch: local temp-plugin CLI proof with `node openclaw.mjs plugins inspect legacy-memory-provider --runtime --json` and text `node openclaw.mjs plugins inspect legacy-memory-provider --runtime`; local `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/status.test.ts src/plugins/compat/registry.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts extensions/openai-compatible-embeddings/src/embedding-provider.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/generic-embedding-provider.integration.test.ts`; local `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check ...`; local `git diff --check`; Testbox `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/status.test.ts src/plugins/compat/registry.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/generic-embedding-provider.integration.test.ts extensions/openai-compatible-embeddings/src/embedding-provider.test.ts`.
Evidence after fix: local CLI inspect reported plugin origin `config` and compatibility code `deprecated-memory-embedding-provider-api`; local focused Vitest passed 5 files / 66 tests; Testbox focused Vitest passed 5 files / 66 tests; oxfmt check and diff whitespace check passed.
Observed result after fix: external/workspace plugins using the old memory embedding provider seam receive a compatibility warning, bundled migration debt stays out of normal user warnings, new bundled legacy registrations are blocked by guardrail allowlists, and the SDK/docs point plugin authors to `api.registerEmbeddingProvider(...)` plus `contracts.embeddingProviders`.
What was not tested: full build and full changed-check suite; a direct remote CLI-inspect attempt was stopped because Testbox source build stayed long-running before the inspect step, so the non-mocked CLI proof was run locally and the focused contract proof was run remotely.
target.author: mbelinky
